name: Cioppinio
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Unpeeled Medium Size Fresh Shrimp
1 1/2 cups Burgundy OR Dry Red Wine
1 3/4 lb Small Clams in Shells, About 16, Scrubbed
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and devein raw shrimp, reserving shells. Skin the fillets, reserving skin.
2. Cut the fillets into 1-inch cubes. Set the shrimp and fillets aside.
3. In a nonaluminum saucepan, combine shrimp shells, fillet skin, water, and vinegar. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 20 minutes. Strain and reserve cooking liquid. Discard fish trimmings.
4. Coat a nonaluminum Dutch oven with cooking spray. Add oil and place over medium-high heat until hot.
5. Add onion and garlic; sauté until tender.
6. Add green pepper and tomatoes, basil, pepper, and red pepper flakes. Cook for 5 minutes.
7. Add wine and reserved cooking liquid; bring to a boil. Reduce heat; simmer uncovered for 15 minutes.
8. Add clams; cook over low heat for 3 minutes or until shells begin to open.
9. Add shrimp cubes, fillet cubes, and crabmeat. Cook over low heat for 7 minutes or until fillet cubes flake easily when tested with a fork.
